records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2209 - Whoever performs four Hajj will never experience the compression of the grave. When they die, Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, will transform the Hajj they performed into a beautiful form, the most excellent of forms, placed before their eyes. It will pray in their grave until Allah (swt) resurrects them, and the reward of that prayer will belong to them. Know that one Rak’ah of that prayer equals a thousand Rak’ahs of the prayer of humans.
records.arabicText: 2209 - وَ مَنْ حَجَّ أَرْبَعَ حِجَجٍ لَمْ تُصِبْهُ ضَغْطَةُ اَلْقَبْرِ أَبَداً وَ إِذَا مَاتَ صَوَّرَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ اَلْحِجَجَ اَلَّتِي حَجَّ فِي صُورَةٍ حَسَنَةٍ أَحْسَنَ مَا يَكُونُ مِنَ اَلصُّوَرِ بَيْنَ عَيْنَيْهِ تُصَلِّي فِي جَوْفِ قَبْرِهِ حَتَّى يَبْعَثَهُ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مِنْ قَبْرِهِ وَ يَكُونُ ثَوَابُ تِلْكَ اَلصَّلاَةِ لَهُ وَ اِعْلَمْ أَنَّ اَلرَّكْعَةَ مِنْ تِلْكَ اَلصَّلاَةِ تَعْدِلُ أَلْفَ رَكْعَةٍ مِنْ صَلاَةِ اَلْآدَمِيِّينَ.

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2210 - Whoever performs five Hajj will never be punished by Allah (swt). Whoever performs ten Hajj will never be subjected to an accounting by Allah (swt). And whoever performs twenty Hajj will never see Hellfire, nor will they hear its roaring or its raging.
records.arabicText: 2210 - وَ مَنْ حَجَّ خَمْسَ حِجَجٍ لَمْ يُعَذِّبْهُ اَللَّهُ أَبَداً وَ مَنْ حَجَّ عَشْرَ حِجَجٍ لَمْ يُحَاسِبْهُ اَللَّهُ أَبَداً وَ مَنْ حَجَّ عِشْرِينَ حَجَّةً لَمْ يَرَ جَهَنَّمَ وَ لَمْ يَسْمَعْ شَهِيقَهَا وَ لاَ زَفِيرَهَا ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2211 - Whoever performs forty Hajj will be told, 'Intercede for whomever you wish,' and a door of Paradise will be opened for them through which they and those for whom they intercede will enter.
records.arabicText: 2211 - وَ مَنْ حَجَّ أَرْبَعِينَ حَجَّةً قِيلَ لَهُ اِشْفَعْ فِيمَنْ أَحْبَبْتَ وَ يُفْتَحُ لَهُ بَابٌ مِنْ أَبْوَابِ اَلْجَنَّةِ يَدْخُلُ مِنْهُ هُوَ وَ مَنْ يَشْفَعُ لَهُ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2212 - Whoever performs fifty Hajj will have a city built for them in the Garden of Eden. Within it will be a thousand palaces, each containing a thousand Houris from the maidens of Paradise and a thousand spouses. They will be made companions of Muhammad, peace and blessings be upon him and his family, in Paradise.
records.arabicText: 2212 - وَ مَنْ حَجَّ خَمْسِينَ حَجَّةً بُنِيَ لَهُ مَدِينَةٌ فِي جَنَّةِ عَدْنٍ فِيهَا أَلْفُ قَصْرٍ فِي كُلِّ قَصْرٍ أَلْفُ حَوْرَاءَ مِنْ حُورِ اَلْعِينِ وَ أَلْفُ زَوْجَةٍ وَ يُجْعَلُ مِنْ رُفَقَاءِ مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فِي اَلْجَنَّةِ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2213 - Whoever performs more than fifty Hajj is like one who performed fifty Hajj alongside Muhammad and the Awsiya (successors), peace be upon them. Such a person will be among those whom Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, visits every Friday and will be among those who enter the Garden of Eden, which Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, created with His own hand, unseen by any eye and unknown to any creature.
For every Hajj they perform, Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, builds a city for them in Paradise. Each city contains chambers, and in each chamber is a Houri from the maidens of Paradise. With every Houri are three hundred maidens, the likes of whom no one has ever seen in beauty and charm.
records.arabicText: 2213 - وَ مَنْ حَجَّ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ خَمْسِينَ حَجَّةً كَانَ كَمَنْ حَجَّ خَمْسِينَ حَجَّةً مَعَ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ اَلْأَوْصِيَاءِ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ كَانَ مِمَّنْ يَزُورُهُ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ كُلَّ جُمُعَةٍ وَ هُوَ مِمَّنْ يَدْخُلُ جَنَّةَ عَدْنٍ اَلَّتِي خَلَقَهَا اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بِيَدِهِ وَ لَمْ تَرَهَا عَيْنٌ وَ لَمْ يَطَّلِعْ عَلَيْهَا مَخْلُوقٌ وَ مَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ يُكْثِرُ اَلْحَجَّ إِلاَّ بَنَى اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَهُ بِكُلِّ حَجَّةٍ مَدِينَةً فِي اَلْجَنَّةِ فِيهَا غُرَفٌ فِي كُلِّ غُرْفَةٍ مِنْهَا حَوْرَاءُ مِنْ حُورِ اَلْعِينِ مَعَ كُلِّ حَوْرَاءَ ثَلاَثُمِائَةِ جَارِيَةٍ لَمْ يَنْظُرِ اَلنَّاسُ إِلَى مِثْلِهِنَّ حُسْناً وَ جَمَالاً ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2214 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "Whoever performs Hajj one year and does not the next, alternating years, is considered among those devoted to Hajj."
records.arabicText: 2214 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «مَنْ حَجَّ سَنَةً وَ سَنَةً لاَ فَهُوَ مِمَّنْ أَدْمَنَ اَلْحَجَّ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2214 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever performs Hajj one year and does not the next, alternating years, is considered among those devoted to Hajj."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2215 - Ishaq ibn Ammar said: I said to Abu Abdullah (Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq), peace be upon him: "I have resolved to perform Hajj every year, either by myself or by sending someone from my household using my wealth."
Imam (as) asked: "Have you firmly decided on this?"
I replied: "Yes, I have resolved to do so."
Imam (as) then said: "If you do this, be certain of the abundance of wealth, or rejoice in the promise of abundant wealth."
records.arabicText: 2215 - وَ قَالَ إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِنِّي قَدْ وَطَّنْتُ نَفْسِي عَلَى لُزُومِ اَلْحَجِّ كُلَّ عَامٍ بِنَفْسِي أَوْ بِرَجُلٍ مِنْ أَهْلِ بَيْتِي بِمَالِي فَقَالَ «وَ قَدْ عَزَمْتَ عَلَى ذَلِكَ» قُلْتُ نَعَمْ قَدْ عَزَمْتُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ فَقَالَ «إِنْ فَعَلْتَ ذَلِكَ فَأَيْقِنْ بِكَثْرَةِ اَلْمَالِ أَوْ أَبْشِرْ بِكَثْرَةِ اَلْمَالِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2215 - Ishaq ibn Ammar said: I said to Abu Abdullah (Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq), peace be upon him:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I have resolved to perform Hajj every year, either by myself or by sending someone from my household using my wealth."
Imam (as) asked: "Have you firmly decided on this?"
I replied: "Yes, I have resolved to do so."
Imam (as) then said: "If you do this, be certain of the abundance of wealth, or rejoice in the promise of abundant wealth."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2216 - It has been narrated: "No servant draws closer to Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, with anything more beloved to Him than walking to His Sacred House on foot. A single Hajj performed in this manner equals seventy Hajj. And whoever alternates between walking and riding their camel, Allah (swt) records for them the reward of both their walking and their riding.
If a pilgrim's sandal strap breaks, Allah (swt) records for them the reward for every step they take barefoot until they wear another sandal."
records.arabicText: 2216 - وَ رُوِيَ : «أَنَّهُ مَا تَقَرَّبَ عَبْدٌ إِلَى اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بِشَيْءٍ أَحَبَّ إِلَيْهِ مِنَ اَلْمَشْيِ إِلَى بَيْتِهِ اَلْحَرَامِ عَلَى اَلْقَدَمَيْنِ وَ إِنَّ اَلْحَجَّةَ اَلْوَاحِدَةَ تَعْدِلُ سَبْعِينَ حَجَّةً وَ مَنْ مَشَى عَنْ جَمَلِهِ كَتَبَ اَللَّهُ لَهُ ثَوَابَ مَا بَيْنَ مَشْيِهِ وَ رُكُوبِهِ وَ اَلْحَاجُّ إِذَا اِنْقَطَعَ شِسْعُ نَعْلِهِ كَتَبَ اَللَّهُ لَهُ ثَوَابَ مَا بَيْنَ مَشْيِهِ حَافِياً إِلَى مُتَنَعِّلٍ ».
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"No servant draws closer to Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, with anything more beloved to Him than walking to His Sacred House on foot. A single Hajj performed in this manner equals seventy Hajj. And whoever alternates between walking and riding their camel, Allah (swt) records for them the reward of both their walking and their riding.
If a pilgrim's sandal strap breaks, Allah (swt) records for them the reward for every step they take barefoot until they wear another sandal."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2217 - "Hajj performed while riding is better than performing it on foot, because the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, performed Hajj while riding."
records.arabicText: 2217 - وَ اَلْحَجُّ رَاكِباً أَفْضَلُ مِنْهُ مَاشِياً لِأَنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ حَجَّ رَاكِباً ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
The reconciliation between these narrations is found in:
Hadith.2218 - Abu Basir narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) where he asked him: "Is walking better or riding?" The Imam (as) replied: "If a man is wealthy and chooses to walk to reduce his expenses, then riding is better."
records.arabicText:
]والجمع ما بين الخبرين في هذا المعنى:[
2018 - مَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو بَصِيرٍ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سَأَلَهُ عَنِ اَلْمَشْيُ أَفْضَلُ أَوِ اَلرُّكُوبُ فَقَالَ «إِذَا كَانَ اَلرَّجُلُ مُوسِراً فَمَشَى لِيَكُونَ أَقَلَّ لِنَفَقَتِهِ فَالرُّكُوبُ أَفْضَلُ.

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2219 - Imam Hussain ibn Ali (as) used to walk while the camels carrying the provisions and supplies were driven alongside him.
records.arabicText: 2219 - وَ كَانَ اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَمْشِي وَ تُسَاقُ مَعَهُ اَلْمَحَامِلُ وَ اَلرِّحَالُ.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam Hussain ibn Ali (as) used to walk while the camels carrying the provisions and supplies were driven alongside him.

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2220 - A man came to Imam Ali ibn Al-Husayn (as) and said:
"You have preferred Hajj over Jihad, while Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, says: 'Indeed, Allah (swt) has purchased from the believers their lives and their wealth in exchange for Paradise'" (Surah At-Tawbah, 9:111).
Imam Ali ibn Al-Husayn (as) replied: "Read what comes after it."
The man continued: 'Those who repent, worship, praise...' (Surah At-Tawbah, 9:112) until he reached the end of the verse.
Then Imam Ali ibn Al-Husayn (as) said: "When you see these qualities (in people), then Jihad alongside them is better than Hajj."
records.arabicText: 2220 - وَ جَاءَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى عَلِيِّ بْنِ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ فَقَالَ قَدْ آثَرْتَ اَلْحَجَّ عَلَى اَلْجِهَادِ وَ قَدْ قَالَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ «۝ إِنَّ اَللّٰهَ اِشْتَرىٰ مِنَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَنْفُسَهُمْ وَ أَمْوٰالَهُمْ بِأَنَّ لَهُمُ اَلْجَنَّةَ ۝» إِلَى آخِرِهَا فَقَالَ لَهُ عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ «فَاقْرَأْ مَا بَعْدَهَا فَقَالَ «۝ اَلتّٰائِبُونَ اَلْعٰابِدُونَ اَلْحٰامِدُونَ ۝» إِلَى أَنْ بَلَغَ آخِرَ اَلْآيَةِ فَقَالَ «إِذَا رَأَيْتَ هَؤُلاَءِ فَالْجِهَادُ مَعَهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ أَفْضَلُ مِنَ اَلْحَجِّ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2221 - Whoever performs Hajj seeking the pleasure of Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, without intending to show off or gain reputation, Allah (swt) will forgive them completely.
records.arabicText: 2221 - وَ مَنْ حَجَّ يُرِيدُ بِهِ وَجْهَ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لاَ يُرِيدُ بِهِ رِيَاءً وَ لاَ سُمْعَةً غَفَرَ اَللَّهُ لَهُ اَلْبَتَّةَ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2222 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said: "Whoever seeks both this world and the Hereafter, let them visit this House."
records.arabicText: 2222 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «مَنْ أَرَادَ دُنْيَا وَ آخِرَةً فَلْيَؤُمَّ هَذَا اَلْبَيْتَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2222 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever seeks both this world and the Hereafter, let them visit this House."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2223 - Whoever returns from Makkah intending to perform Hajj again the following year will have their lifespan increased.
records.arabicText: 2223 - وَ مَنْ رَجَعَ مِنْ مَكَّةَ وَ هُوَ يَنْوِي اَلْحَجَّ مِنْ قَابِلٍ زِيدَ فِي عُمُرِهِ ».
records.thaqalaynMatn: Whoever returns from Makkah intending to perform Hajj again the following year will have their lifespan increased.

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2224 - Whoever departs from Makkah without intending to return has drawn near to their death, and their punishment approaches.
records.arabicText: 2224 - وَ مَنْ خَرَجَ مِنْ مَكَّةَ وَ هُوَ لاَ يَنْوِي اَلْعَوْدَ إِلَيْهَا فَقَدْ قَرُبَ أَجَلُهُ وَ دَنَا عَذَابُهُ ».
records.thaqalaynMatn: Whoever departs from Makkah without intending to return has drawn near to their death, and their punishment approaches.

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2225 - It is narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that he said: "Do you see this mountain, Thafil? When Yazid ibn Muawiyah returned from his Hajj and set out toward Sham, he composed these verses:
When we leave Thafil to our right,
We shall never return for years to come,
Neither for Hajj nor Umrah while we live.
Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, caused him to die before his appointed time."
records.arabicText:
2225 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «تَرَوْنَ هَذَا اَلْجَبَلَ ثَافِلاً إِنَّ يَزِيدَ بْنَ مُعَاوِيَةَ لَمَّا رَجَعَ مِنْ حَجِّهِ مُرْتَحِلاً إِلَى اَلشَّامِ أَنْشَأَ يَقُولُ:
إِذَا تَرَكْنَا ثَافِلاً يَمِيناً
فَلَنْ نَعُودَ بَعْدَهُ سِنِينَا
لِلْحَجِّ وَ اَلْعُمْرَةِ مَا بَقِينَا
فَأَمَاتَهُ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ قَبْلَ أَجَلِهِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2225 - It is narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Do you see this mountain, Thafil? When Yazid ibn Muawiyah returned from his Hajj and set out toward Sham, he composed these verses:
When we leave Thafil to our right,
We shall never return for years to come,
Neither for Hajj nor Umrah while we live.
Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, caused him to die before his appointed time."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2226 - Abu Ja’far (as) said: "No servant prioritizes a worldly need over performing Hajj except that they will see the pilgrims who have shaved their heads return before their own need is fulfilled."
records.arabicText: 2226 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «مَا مِنْ عَبْدٍ يُؤْثِرُ عَلَى اَلْحَجِّ حَاجَةً مِنْ حَوَائِجِ اَلدُّنْيَا إِلاَّ نَظَرَ إِلَى اَلْمُحَلِّقِينَ قَدِ اِنْصَرَفُوا قَبْلَ أَنْ تُقْضَى لَهُ تِلْكَ اَلْحَاجَةُ ».
records.thaqalaynMatn: "No servant prioritizes a worldly need over performing Hajj except that they will see the pilgrims who have shaved their heads return before their own need is fulfilled."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2227 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "No man refrains from performing Hajj except due to a sin, yet Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, forgives more than He holds back."
records.arabicText: 2227 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «مَا تَخَلَّفَ رَجُلٌ مِنَ اَلْحَجِّ إِلاَّ بِذَنْبٍ وَ مَا يَعْفُو اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَكْثَرُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2227 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "No man refrains from performing Hajj except due to a sin, yet Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, forgives more than He holds back."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2228 - He was asked about the words of Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic: "So I will give in charity and be among the righteous" (Surah Al-Munafiqun 63:10).
Imam (as) said: "'I will give in charity' refers to giving charity, and 'be among the righteous' means performing Hajj."
records.arabicText: 2228 - وَ سُئِلَ عَنْ قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ «۝ فَأَصَّدَّقَ وَ أَكُنْ مِنَ اَلصّٰالِحِينَ ۝» قَالَ «أَصَّدَّقَ مِنَ اَلصَّدَقَةِ «وَ أَكُنْ مِنَ اَلصّٰالِحِينَ» أَيْ أَحُجَّ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2229 - Imam (as) said: "Umrah to Umrah is an expiation for what occurs between them."
records.arabicText: 2229 - وَ قَالَ : «اَلْعُمْرَةُ إِلَى اَلْعُمْرَةِ كَفَّارَةُ مَا بَيْنَهُمَا».
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Umrah to Umrah is an expiation for what occurs between them."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2230 - It is narrated from the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him and his family: "The reward for Hajj is Paradise, and Umrah is an expiation for all sins. The best Umrah is the Umrah performed in the month of Rajab."
records.arabicText: 2230 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ قَالَ : «اَلْحَجَّةُ ثَوَابُهَا اَلْجَنَّةُ وَ اَلْعُمْرَةُ كَفَّارَةُ كُلِّ ذَنْبٍ وَ أَفْضَلُ اَلْعُمْرَةِ عُمْرَةُ رَجَبٍ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2230 - It is narrated from the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him and his family:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The reward for Hajj is Paradise, and Umrah is an expiation for all sins. The best Umrah is the Umrah performed in the month of Rajab."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2231 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said: "Every blessing will be questioned about by its owner, except for that which was used in battle or Hajj."
records.arabicText: 2231 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «كُلُّ نَعِيمٍ مَسْئُولٌ عَنْهُ صَاحِبُهُ إِلاَّ مَا كَانَ فِي غَزْوٍ أَوْ حَجٍّ».
records.thaqalaynMatn: The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said: "Every blessing will be questioned about by its owner, except for that which was used in battle or Hajj."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2232 - Abu Ja’far Imam Al-Baqir (as) said: "Hajj and Umrah are two marketplaces from the marketplaces of the Hereafter. Those who adhere to them are the guests of Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic. If He keeps them alive, they are kept without sin; and if He causes them to die, He enters them into Paradise."
records.arabicText: 2232 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ اَلْبَاقِرُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «اَلْحَجُّ وَ اَلْعُمْرَةُ سُوقَانِ مِنْ أَسْوَاقِ اَلْآخِرَةِ اَللاَّزِمُ لَهُمَا مِنْ أَضْيَافِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِنْ أَبْقَاهُ أَبْقَاهُ وَ لاَ ذَنْبَ لَهُ وَ إِنْ أَمَاتَهُ أَدْخَلَهُ اَلْجَنَّةَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2232 - Abu Ja’far Imam Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Hajj and Umrah are two marketplaces from the marketplaces of the Hereafter. Those who adhere to them are the guests of Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic. If He keeps them alive, they are kept without sin; and if He causes them to die, He enters them into Paradise."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2233 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about a man in debt who borrows money to perform Hajj.
Imam (as) said: "Yes, it is more likely to help him repay his debt."
records.arabicText: 2233 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ رَجُلٍ ذِي دَيْنٍ يَسْتَدِينُ وَ يَحُجُّ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ هُوَ أَقْضَى لِلدَّيْنِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2233 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about a man in debt who borrows money to perform Hajj.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Yes, it is more likely to help him repay his debt."
